properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued July 22 at 10:59PM MDT until July 23 at 2:00AM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
* WHAT...Arroyo and small stream flooding caused by excessive
rainfall is expected. This area of rainfall is moving over areas
that have already received heavy rain earlier today.

* WHERE...A portion of southwest Colorado, including the following
county, San Miguel.

* WHEN...Until 200 AM MDT.

* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 1057 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated heavy rain due to
thunderstorms. This will cause arroyo and small stream
flooding. Between 0.2 and 0.5 inches of rain have fallen.
- Some locations that will experience flooding include...
Norwood, Sawpit, Placerville and Fall Creek and areas along
Highway 145.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued July 22 at 10:53PM MDT until July 23 at 2:00AM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by excessive rainfall is expected. This
area of rainfall is moving over areas that have already received
heavy rain earlier today.

* WHERE...A portion of northwest Colorado, including the following
county, Garfield.

* WHEN...Until 200 AM MDT.

* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas.
Mudslides or debris flows are possible. Avoid traveling and
recreating through the advisory area.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 1053 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated heavy rain due to
thunderstorms. Minor flooding is ongoing or expected to begin
shortly in the advisory area. Between 0.2 and 0.5 inches of
rain have fallen.
- Additional rainfall amounts of 0.3 to 0.5 inches are expected
over the area. This additional rain will result in minor
flooding.
- Some locations that will experience flooding include...
Glenwood Springs, Cattle Creek and Shoshone.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 22 at 10:48PM MDT until July 22 at 11:45PM MDT by NWS Denver CO
properties.description:
At 1048 PM MDT, Doppler radar and automated rain gauges indicated
thunderstorms producing heavy rain across the warned area. Between
1.5 and 4 inches of rain have fallen. Flash flooding is ongoing or
expected to begin shortly.

HAZARD...Flash flooding caused by thunderstorms.

SOURCE...Radar and automated gauges.

IMPACT...Flash flooding of small creeks and streams, gulches, roads,
and poor drainage and low-lying areas.

Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
Hugo, Arriba and Genoa.

This includes the following streams and drainages...
Hell Creek, Long Creek, Dugout Creek, Big Sandy Creek, Sevenmile
Creek and South Fork Republican River.

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued July 22 at 10:46PM MDT until July 23 at 1:45AM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
* WHAT...Flash flooding and debris flows are possible, flooding
caused by excessive rainfall over the Gold Mountain Burn Scar is
possible.

* WHERE...Gold Mountain Burn Scar in Ouray County Colorado,
including Dexter and Cutler Creek drainages and Highway 550.

* WHEN...Until 145 AM MDT.

* IMPACTS...Excessive rainfall is possible over the Gold Mountain
Burn Scar which could lead to flash flooding and/or debris flows,
particularly along highway 550 between Cutler Creek and Dexter
Creek. The debris flow can consist of rock, mud, vegetation and
other loose materials.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 1046 PM MDT, At 1045 PM MDT, The probability of heavy
rainfall will
increase through at least midnight. If thunderstorms develop
over the Gold Mountain burn scar, debris flows are likely
within Cutler and Dexter Creek drainages, including Highway
550.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood
